metadata.operator_override: v3 combined verdict was AUDITED (review 44/44 advance:true, audit_clean:true); the step-2 early exit was operator-waived because the spec_ref oracle changed underneath the primer: whitepaper PR #901 + ADR 0006 (docs/decisions/0006-pq-architecture-ratification.md) replaced ML-DSA-65 minting signatures with PoW-preimage attribution binding, so v3 §6 now contradicts the corrected spec. This v4 is the operator-directed spec-consistency restoration scoped by GitHub issue #905 (+ its comments); no other changes.
metadata.spec_ref_note: Project root moved from whitepaper/primer/ to docs/primer/; the BRIEF's spec_ref glob is now ../../whitepaper/sections/*.tex and re-resolves to the same 18 sections, post-PR-#901 content. Governing text for this revision: 04-cryptography.tex §Minting Attribution (lines 340–365), 03-preliminaries.tex lines 149–157 (designated future signature family), tab:hybrid row 'Minting attribution / Permanent / PoW hash binding'.

dependency_order_delta: §6 minting re-teach uses only §2-toolbox hashes + the pre-existing '(Section 8)' mining forward-pointer the v3 critics accepted as bridgeable
§10 step 8 points back to §6 for the zero-signature attribution
§11 caveat uses only §5/§6 vocabulary, both taught long before
spec_ref_grounding_delta: New §6 teaching beat matches WP §4 Minting Attribution: preimage commits to minter keys; reattribution destroys the PoW against an externalized chain; Grover-only quantum exposure; no signature; ~5.3 KB/block saved (3,309 B sig + 1,952 B pk = 5,261 B)
ML-DSA-65 survives only as the designated future signature family (WP §3) — grep-verified: exactly two deliberate mentions remain (§6 parenthetical, §11 FIPS 204 pointer)
§11 caveat facts (public amounts today, no ML-KEM ciphertexts today, QP tier retired) verified against ADR 0006 context + decisions 1/2/4
technical_accuracy_delta: fig2 minting box now 'PoW-preimage binding (hash-based), zero signature bytes' and PERM subgraph header no longer claims lattice for the minting leg
fig5 step 8 drops ML-DSA-65; 'identity bound by the PoW preimage — no signature'
No remaining claim anywhere in body or exhibits that minting transactions are signed

figures_carried_forward.note: All 10 exhibit files copied from v3 so relative refs resolve; fig1/fig3/fig4 (.mmd + .png) byte-identical carries. fig2-hybrid-pq-envelope and fig5-capstone-payment-timeline re-authored (.mmd) per #905/ADR 0006 and re-rendered (.png) in this phase via mmdc 11.15.0 (anvil mermaid theme at .anvil/anvil/lib/figures/mermaid-theme.json, -b white -s 2, system-Chrome puppeteer executablePath override); both re-renders visually verified (theme classes intact, text legible, no clipping). v3 remains untouched.

metadata.pdf_note: No PDF in this version by design; primer-figures runs after the v4 critic pair (with the #690-thread render hardening: 0.85\textheight cap, labelformat=empty, source-driven glyph sweep).

figures.rendered_note: No diagrams re-rendered in this phase by design: all five exhibits were already present in the v4 version dir (fig2/fig5 re-rendered during the v4 revise per #905/ADR 0006; fig1/fig3/fig4 byte-identical carries from v3). Presence verified; recorded as rendered.

gate.audited_gate: review 44/44 ADVANCE (botho-from-the-basics.4.review) + audit CLEAN (botho-from-the-basics.4.audit) confirmed before render
